children.name: Chaetops
children.description: A genus of two medium-sized, ground-dwelling passerines endemic to the mountainous regions of southern Africa, known as rockjumpers for their habit of hopping and clambering over rocky outcrops and boulders. They have strong legs and feet, a long tail that is frequently cocked, and a slightly decurved bill. Their plumage combines warm rufous, grey, and white in a distinctive pattern.
